skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Video outpainting on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI - extend the spatial canvas of a video, change aspect ratio (9:16 to 16:9 or vice versa), and add environment beyond the original frame while preserving the central action.
skill.summary: Extend video spatial canvas and change aspect ratios using AI-powered outpainting via the runcomfy CLI.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 64 static analysis findings evaluated as FALSE POSITIVES. The 33 external_commands findings are documentation code blocks showing legitimate runcomfy CLI usage constrained by allowed-tools. The 22 network URL findings are hardcoded references to the legitimate RunComfy platform (runcomfy.com, skills.sh, docs.runcomfy.com). The 2 filesystem findings document the CLI standard token storage location. No actual malicious patterns or code execution vectors detected. The skill is a well-documented reference for the RunComfy video outpainting service with proper security considerations documented.

medium_findings.title: Shell backtick execution in code blocks
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 33 instances of backtick-execution patterns. All instances are documentation code blocks showing legitimate runcomfy CLI commands (runcomfy run, runcomfy login). The skill is constrained by allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *) which limits execution to the runcomfy CLI only. No arbitrary command execution risk. FALSE POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.85 - strong contextual evidence these are markdown documentation examples, not executable injection points.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to RunComfy platform
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 22 hardcoded URL patterns. The runcomfy CLI communicates only with model-api.runcomfy.net as documented in the Security and Privacy section. No data exfiltration risk. FALSE POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.90 - all URLs are well-documented platform references with consistent domain patterns.

low_findings.title: Hidden file and token storage documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 148 for hidden file access (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). This is documentation explaining where the runcomfy CLI stores its authentication token with mode 0600 permissions. The skill does not actively read or exfiltrate this file. FALSE POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.95 - the text is clearly in the Security and Privacy documentation section explaining standard CLI behavior.

content.user_title: Extend video canvas with AI outpainting
content.value_statement: Video outpainting typically requires complex ComfyUI setups and manual workflow configuration. This skill lets you extend video spatial canvas and change aspect ratios through simple runcomfy CLI commands with AI-powered Wan 2-7 models.
actual_capabilities: Extend a video spatial canvas to change aspect ratio between 9:16 vertical and 16:9 horizontal
Invoke Wan 2-7 Edit-Video through the runcomfy CLI for prompt-shaped spatial extension
Route to dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows for hero-grade seam quality
Apply prompting strategies that preserve central subject framing and extend background
Convert between common formats: TikTok vertical to YouTube horizontal, square to wide
Handle multi-step outpainting with sequential passes for large canvas expansions
limitations: Wan 2-7 Edit-Video produces quality variance at seam boundaries between original and extended areas
Dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows require GUI access and cannot be invoked via CLI
Multi-step outpainting quality degrades significantly after two sequential passes
Does not support image outpainting, video temporal extension, or mask-driven inpainting

use_cases.title: Social media content adaptation
use_cases.description: A social media manager converts 9:16 vertical TikTok clips to 16:9 horizontal YouTube format while preserving central action and adding matching background on both sides.

use_cases.title: Legacy footage modernization
use_cases.description: A video editor uncrops old 4:3 archival footage to modern 16:9 widescreen format for a documentary project using AI-generated environment extension.

use_cases.title: Brand content reformatting
use_cases.description: A content creator extends square Instagram Reels to wide brand banner format for website and display advertising with consistent background styling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic aspect ratio swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend my vertical video from 9:16 to 16:9 horizontal by adding matching environment on the left and right sides. Preserve the original action and subject framing in the center.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting a TikTok video to YouTube format

prompt_templates.title: Directional canvas extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video canvas downward to reveal more ground and foreground. Match the existing ground texture, lighting direction, and camera angle. Preserve the original subject position in the upper portion of the frame.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding more foreground detail to a landscape video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step uncrop for large expansion
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply a first-pass extension of 30% to the left and right sides of this video. After the first pass completes, apply a second pass to extend another 20%. Use the same background style and lighting throughout. Preserve the central action at every step.
prompt_templates.scenario: Significantly widening a narrow video beyond single-pass capability

prompt_templates.title: Hero-grade outpainting via ComfyUI workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: This video requires strict temporal seam consistency for a commercial ad. Open the LTX 2-3 outpainting ComfyUI workflow at runcomfy.com. Use the outpainting graph with motion-aware fill for spatial frame expansion with strict seam handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: High-quality ad delivery where seam quality is critical

output_examples.input: How do I convert a 9:16 TikTok video to 16:9 YouTube format?
output_examples.output: The skill provides step-by-step instructions to invoke the Wan 2-7 Edit-Video model with a prompt that describes extending the canvas horizontally while matching background style, lighting, and camera distance. The runcomfy CLI handles model API calls, status polling, and downloads the result to the specified output directory. The result is a 16:9 video with the original subject centered and AI-generated environment filling the sides.

output_examples.input: Can I improve the seam quality of my outpainted video?
output_examples.output: The skill recommends using dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows hosted on RunComfy for hero-grade quality. These workflows provide better temporal consistency and motion-aware fill compared to Wan 2-7 Edit-Video. The ComfyUI workflows require GUI access in the RunComfy cloud environment and are linked with detailed descriptions of their capabilities for different use cases.

best_practices: Always include preservation language in your prompt such as preserve the original action and subject framing to prevent Wan from restyling central content.
Describe the extending environment with specific details about background style, lighting, depth of field, and camera distance for coherent results.
Use dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows for commercial ad delivery where seam quality and temporal consistency are critical.
anti_patterns: Omitting preservation language in the prompt causes Wan to restyle or reinterpret the central subject and action in unintended ways.
Applying more than two sequential outpaint passes degrades quality significantly with each iteration and produces visible artifacts.
Using Wan 2-7 Edit-Video for footage that requires strict temporal seam consistency such as hero advertisements or professional deliverables.

faq.question: What is video outpainting?
faq.answer: Video outpainting extends the spatial canvas of a video beyond its original frame, adding new AI-generated visual content to the sides, top, or bottom while preserving the central action.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for image outpainting?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for video outpainting only. For image outpainting on single still images, use the dedicated image-outpainting skill instead.

faq.question: What is the difference between Route 1 and ComfyUI workflows?
faq.answer: Route 1 uses Wan 2-7 Edit-Video via the CLI for quick prompt-shaped extension. ComfyUI workflows provide higher quality with better temporal consistency but require GUI access in the RunComfy cloud.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with the runcomfy CLI?
faq.answer: Run runcomfy login for interactive authentication or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and container environments. The token is stored in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json.

faq.question: What aspect ratio conversions does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports converting between 9:16 vertical and 16:9 horizontal, square Instagram to wide banner, and 4:3 to 16:9 widescreen formats.

faq.question: Why does my outpainted video have visible seams?
faq.answer: Wan 2-7 Edit-Video was not specifically trained for outpainting, so seam quality varies. For strict seam quality requirements, use the dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows.
